_comment: Resources that must be created manually in the Databricks UI or GCP Console because their creation is not fully automatable via the Databricks CLI or REST API. Equivalent to the Fabric manual-resources.json.

manual_resources.name: GCS Pub/Sub Cloud Storage Export Subscription
manual_resources.why_manual: gcloud CLI setup; requires GCP project permissions outside this repo.
steps: gsutil mb -l <REGION> gs://<GRIZL_GCS_LOGS_BUCKET>

gcloud pubsub subscriptions create grizl-logs-gcs-export --topic=<PUBSUB_TOPIC> --project=<GCP_PROJECT_ID> --cloud-storage-bucket=<GRIZL_GCS_LOGS_BUCKET> --cloud-storage-file-prefix=logs/ --cloud-storage-file-suffix=.jsonl --cloud-storage-max-duration=60s --cloud-storage-output-format=text
gsutil iam ch serviceAccount:<DATABRICKS_GSA>@<GCP_PROJECT_ID>.iam.gserviceaccount.com:objectViewer gs://<GRIZL_GCS_LOGS_BUCKET>
Or use: GCS_BUCKET=<bucket> npm --prefix databricks run gcs-subscription
NOTE: the existing pull subscription is consumed by grizl-log-forwarder; the new gcs-export subscription runs in parallel, zero forwarder changes required

manual_resources.name: Databricks SQL Warehouse
manual_resources.why_manual: Warehouse creation requires capacity allocation decisions (size, auto-stop, type).
steps: Open Databricks SQL → SQL Warehouses → Create warehouse
Choose Serverless or Classic (Serverless recommended for lower cold-start latency)
Set auto-stop to 10 minutes
Copy the warehouse ID into DATABRICKS_WAREHOUSE_ID

manual_resources.name: DLT Auto Loader Pipeline
manual_resources.why_manual: Deployed via databricks bundle deploy after configuring databricks/databricks.yml.
steps: Set variables in databricks/databricks.yml (gcs_logs_path, incident_webhook_url)
databricks bundle deploy --target dev
Copy the DLT pipeline ID into DATABRICKS_DLT_PIPELINE_ID
Start the pipeline: Workflows → Delta Live Tables → grizl-autoloader-pipeline → Start

manual_resources.name: Genie Space (AI/BI Genie)
manual_resources.why_manual: Genie space creation and table configuration is UI-only.
steps: Open Databricks → AI/BI → Genie → New space
Title: grizl-observability
Add tables from databricks/manifests/genie-space-settings.example.json
Add example questions from the same file
Publish the space
Copy the space ID from the URL into DATABRICKS_GENIE_SPACE_ID
Smoke-test: bash databricks/scripts/genie-mgmt.sh ask 'What are the recent errors for grizl-backend?'

manual_resources.name: Lakehouse Monitor
manual_resources.why_manual: Created by running notebooks/02_lakehouse_monitor_setup.py once in the workspace.
steps: Open notebooks/02_lakehouse_monitor_setup.py in the Databricks workspace
Attach to a cluster with Databricks Runtime 13.3 LTS+
Run all cells
Verify output tables: grizl.observability_monitors.raw_logs_profile
Set up SQL Alerts on drift metrics (see sql/grizl-alert-queries.sql Alert 8)

manual_resources.name: Databricks Service Principal
manual_resources.why_manual: Requires account-level admin access to create service principals.
steps: Open Databricks account console → Service principals → Add service principal
Generate a client secret
Grant the SP workspace access with 'User' role
Grant Unity Catalog permissions: USE CATALOG grizl, USE SCHEMA observability, SELECT on all views and tables
Store client_id in DATABRICKS_CLIENT_ID (non-secret)
Store client_secret in GCP Secret Manager as DATABRICKS_CLIENT_SECRET

manual_resources.name: Databricks SQL Alerts
manual_resources.why_manual: SQL Alert creation and webhook destination setup is UI-based.
steps: Open Databricks SQL → Alerts → Create alert
For each query in sql/grizl-alert-queries.sql, create a corresponding alert
Alert action option A (Slack): create a webhook destination pointing at your Slack incoming webhook URL

Alert action option B (backend webhook): configure webhook destination URL = https://<backend>/api/databricks/incidents with header x-grizl-databricks-secret — only if a backend incident endpoint is implemented
The Workflow job (notebooks/04_anomaly_signals_workflow.py) is the preferred action path — it creates GitHub issues directly and does not require a backend webhook endpoint
Enable the alert
manual_resources.notes: The grizl-backend already has a telemetry sink at POST /api/telemetry (frontend browser events) and a LangGraph-based chat quality agent (apps/agent-runtime/) for anomaly detection on the chat flow. Infrastructure anomaly actions from Databricks are separate and can go directly to GitHub or Slack without requiring a backend webhook endpoint.
